title: Pet Care Information - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Basic Needs > Pet Care Information 2 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Foster Families Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF PORTLAND ANIMAL WELFARE TEAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides veterinary health care clinics by appointment and access to a pet supply bank. Households can access the pet supply bank monthly without appointments or pre-qualification. Veterinary care is offered by appointments or walk-in pre-scheduled drop-in clinic dates. Dates of drop-in clinics are available on website and phone voicemail. Information can also be provided in person during regular hours. Does not offer emergency, urgent, or walk-in veterinary care outside of pre-scheduled drop-in clinic dates. At veterinary clinics, maximum of two pets per household will be seen. Services include: -Basic health exams -Vaccines -Flea and de-worming treatments -Pet supplies -Discounted animal licensing Accepts donations of new and gently used pet supplies and some medications. Check website for specific donation requirements. Cannot accept: Any food past one year of expiration, CBD treats or supplements, retractable leashes, shock collars, electric collars, or prong collars. Volunteer opportunities are available. 1718 NE 82nd Avenue Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Pets of people who are experiencing homelessness or extreme poverty. Monthly income must be below the federal poverty level. Pets must be spayed/neutered or client must be willing to spay/neuter their pet. County Licensing: Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington County residents Volunteers: Age 16 and older Hours: Tuesday-Thursday 10am-3pm Veterinary care: By appointment only Vaccine and Wellness Clinics: Varies Email (503) 206-6033 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROGUE VALLEY HUMANE SOCIETY PET RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers pet resources including trap/neuter/return (TNR) for community cats, pet food, humane education on responsible ownership and pet awareness, and lost and found pet assistance. Delivery of pet food once a month to eligible pet owners. 429 NW Scenic Drive Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Most services: Unrestricted Pet food: Low-income pet owners Delivery: Homebound seniors, veterans, and individuals with disabilities Hours: Monday-Saturday noon-4pm Email (541) 479-5154 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND ANIMAL WELFARE TEAM PORTLAND ANIMAL WELFARE TEAM ROGUE VALLEY HUMANE SOCIETY PET RESOURCES
